sections.paragraph:
σαρκοφόρος, ον (σάρξ + φέρω via φόρος; Nicetas Eugen. 3, 319 Hercher; of humans SibOr 8, 222; of Christ 1, 325; Clem. of Alex., Strom. 5, 34, 1) flesh-bearing of Christ’s appearing in true human form ὁμολογεῖν αὐτὸν (=κύριον) σαρκοφόρον confess that he was clothed in flesh (adj. or subst.) ISm 5:2.—Cp. forms s.v. φέρω in DELG p. 1190.

Nicetas Eugen Nicetas Eugen , XII a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

adj. adj. = adjective

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 914
